summary.title: DU Hockey Ticket Management System
summary.description: Threat model for the DU Hockey Ticket Management System, a Flask-based web application with registration, login, ticket purchasing, refund requests, admin management, SQLite database storage, and session/token-based authentication.

data.name: User / Admin Authorization Boundary
data.description: Separates normal authenticated user functionality from privileged administrative functionality. Regular users should not be able to cross this boundary or perform admin-only actions.

data.name:
Registered
User
data.description: Authenticated regular user who can access user-only features such as browsing available games, reserving seats, purchasing tickets, viewing issued tickets/barcodes, and submitting refund requests for tickets they own.

data.name: Admin User
data.description: Authenticated administrator who can access the admin dashboard, manage users, suspend/reactivate accounts, elevate user roles, manage tickets, and approve or deny refund requests.

data.name:
Anonymous
Visitor
data.description: Unauthenticated visitor who can view public pages, register for an account, and attempt to log in.

data.name:
Browse
Games
data.description: Allows users to view available hockey games, sections, pricing, and seat availability before making a ticket reservation or purchase.

data.name:
Login/Register
+
Session Valiation
data.description: Handles account registration, login, password verification, CSRF validation, session/token creation, and authentication checks for protected routes.

data.name:
Ticket
Purchase
data.description: Handles ticket reservation and purchase logic, including game selection, section selection, quantity validation, student discount checks, payment method submission, ticket creation, order creation, and barcode generation.

data.name:
Refund
Request
data.description: Allows authenticated users to submit refund requests for tickets they own. The process verifies ticket ownership, prevents duplicate refund requests, and stores refund request status for admin review.

data.name:
Admin
Management
data.description: Handles privileged administrative actions such as viewing users, suspending or reactivating accounts, elevating users to admin, reviewing refund requests, approving or denying refunds, and managing ticket-related records.

data.name:
Application / Database
Trust Boundary
data.description: Separates the Flask application logic from the SQLite database. Data crossing this boundary should be validated, authorized, and safely queried or updated through trusted server-side logic.

data.name: Credentials + CSRF
data.description: The anonymous visitor submits registration or login data to the application. This may include username, email address, password, password confirmation, and CSRF token. This flow crosses the Internet/Application Trust Boundary and must be protected against credential theft, brute force attacks, CSRF, injection, and malformed input.

data.name: User lookup/write
data.description: The authentication process reads and writes user account records in the database. During registration, it creates a new user record and stores the password hash. During login, it looks up the submitted email or username, verifies the password hash, and checks account status and role information.

data.name: Secret key read
data.description: The authentication/session process reads sensitive configuration values such as SECRET_KEY or token-signing secrets to create, sign, or validate sessions/tokens. Exposure or weakness of these secrets could allow session forgery, impersonation, or privilege escalation.

data.name: Game browsing
data.description: An authenticated user requests available hockey games, sections, pricing, and seating information. This flow should only expose information appropriate for regular users and should not reveal internal database details, hidden admin information, or sensitive system state.

data.name: Game/seat lookup
data.description: The application queries the database for available games, teams, sections, seat availability, and pricing information. These queries support the user-facing game browsing and ticket selection pages.

data.name: Game list
data.description: The application returns game listings, section options, seat availability, and pricing information to the authenticated user. The response should be properly encoded and should not expose unnecessary internal database fields or debugging information.

data.name: Session cookie
data.description: After successful authentication, the application returns an authenticated session cookie or token to the user’s browser. This value allows the user to access protected application features. It must be protected with secure session settings such as HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, expiration, and server-side validation.

data.name:
Internet/
Application Trust Boundary
data.description: Separates untrusted browser/client traffic from the Flask application. Requests crossing this boundary must be authenticated, authorized, validated, and protected against tampering.

data.name: SQLite Database
data.description: Persistent application database storing users, roles, password hashes, games, teams, sections, seats, section pricing, orders, tickets, payments, barcodes, and refund requests.

data.name:
Application Secrets/
Environment Variables
data.description: Runtime configuration and sensitive values used by the Flask application, including SECRET_KEY, admin seed credentials, database configuration, and other environment-specific settings.
